content:
3 min read
1 hour ago

--

As a hybrid dev/designer who recently shifted deeper into the UX world, I’ve built more portfolio sites than I can count — React, plain HTML/CSS, WordPress, custom builds, you name it.
But I’d never created a portfolio focused strictly on UX design. And when a freelance opportunity popped up last minute, I needed something fast.
That’s how I landed on UXfolio, a platform promising to “build the UX portfolio that gets you hired.” I expected another drag‑and‑drop builder with rigid templates and limited customization. Instead, I was genuinely surprised.
First Impressions
UXfolio’s template library immediately stood out. I usually struggle to find a template I like, but this time I had trouble choosing between several.
I ultimately went with Norman, a clean, modern layout that felt aligned with my design style.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

UXfolio template selectorAfter selecting a template, I was directed to the builder page, where a user can edit the page layout, design, text, and case studies.
Once inside the builder, the experience was refreshingly intuitive. The left‑panel design controls offered more customization than I expected — preset color palettes, palette generation from a base color, beautiful typography options, and the ability to switch templates mid‑build without nuking your layout.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

UXfolio web builderBuilding the Case Studies
Up next are the case studies. There are a variety of pre‑designed sections for intros, process steps, and conclusions. The layouts were thoughtfully crafted and surprisingly adaptable. Padding, columns, widths, and design elements were all adjustable.
I also appreciated the thumbnail frame options, even though I ended up using my own assets.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Case study builder: OutlinePress enter or click to view image in full size

Case study builder: ThumbnailThe Experience
I expected the usual drag‑and‑drop frustrations (iykyk), but UXfolio was genuinely fun to use. The builder felt smooth, predictable, and flexible enough that I never felt boxed in.
And because I was on a tight deadline, the pre‑built layouts were truly a lifesaver. Instead of spending days searching for inspiration, I could focus on writing strong case study content.
In total, the portfolio took me about 6–8 hours, and most of that time was spent writing the actual content, not using the tool.
The Final Result
Here’s how my portfolio turned out — clean, cohesive, and something I’d be proud to send to clients.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

My UXfolio PortfolioView the full portfolio here: Liz Atkins | UX Designer
My Honest Rating

• Usability: 10/10

• Customization: 9/10

• Case Study Tools: 10/10

• Speed of Build: 9/10

• Overall Experience: 9.5/10
Final Thoughts
Will it be “the UX portfolio that gets me hired”? Time will tell (I sent it off today). But as a fast, intuitive, and genuinely designer‑friendly platform, UXfolio exceeded my expectations. If you’re a UX designer who needs a polished portfolio without sinking days into development, it’s absolutely worth trying.
